title: Curriculum Vitae
text:
Research and Publications
Optimal Portfolio Construction | May 2026 - Present

Lead Author – Working paper. Optimal portfolio construction.

Adaptive Execution Strategies Lead to Robust Improvements Over Naive Benchmark

Lead Author – Designed an adaptive execution strategy using L2 order book data and an execution simulator to test it. The simulator can simulate the fills and impact of any generic fill strategy using market orders, resting limit orders, and IOC limit orders in the presences of variable latency, and book resilience (modeled as a Poisson process). Final strategy offered large improvements over naive strategies, using microstructure signals to adapt fill agrression.

title: Curriculum Vitae
text:
Work Experience
Deloitte Consulting | Austin, TX
Deloitte Quantum AI Analyst | Jan 2023 - Dec 2024

Client Pitching – Pitched the firm’s quantum computation and optimization capabilities in presentations to commercial clients from the finance and logistics sectors.
Quantum Optimization – Built and presented Python demonstrations of Grover’s quantum search algorithm using IBM Qiskit in Python and IBM simulators and quantum hardware.

Business Technology Analyst, AI and Data | Oct 2022 - Jun 2025

Tech Infrastructure – Built modern web infrastructure for the Texas Office of the Inspector General, allowing them to more effectively manage, report, and archive $140,000,000 of annual Medicaid fraud recoveries.
Team Leadership – Led a team of four software developers and testers and shipped a case processing system for Texas OIG to recover $10,000,000 of previously unrecoverable fraudulent claims upon deployment in 2023.

title: Curriculum Vitae

Millennium x Columbia University Trading Competition | New York, NY
ETF Arbitrage Simulation | Aug 2025

Leadership – Led a team of four at an Millennium Managment ETF arbitrage competition for Mathematics of Finance students. Achieved 1st place measured by risk-adjusted returns.

title: Research and Publications
text:
Adaptive Execution Strategies Lead to Robust Improvements Over Naive Benchmark



Market Microstructure

Execution



Designed an adaptive execution strategy using L2 order book data and an execution simulator to test it. The simulator can simulate the fills and impact of any generic fill strategy using market orders, resting limit orders, and IOC limit orders in the presence of variable latency and book resilience (modeled as a Poisson process). The final strategy offered large improvements over naive strategies, using microstructure signals to adapt fill aggression.

title: Michael Cusack-Nelkin
text: I am a master’s student in the Mathematics of Finance (MAFN) program at Columbia University. Prior to joining Columbia, I worked at Deloitte Consulting LLP for three years. My research focuses on constructing optimal equity portfolios using return forecasts, covariance and volatility estimates, and trading cost models. My favorite things to do outside of research are to read The Lord of the Rings, solve problems on Project Euler, boulder, and hangout with my wife and puppy.
